instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Here Is Everything You Need to Know About Section 80C
For a taxpayer, Section 80C is probably the ideal part of paying taxes. Under this section of the Income Tax Act, of 1961, there are a number of activities that offer deductions in the taxes that one has to pay. While 80C is quite popular, there are many aspects that one may not be completely aware of. Understanding all the deductions in detail can affect your tax exemptions to a extent and can help you save up to INR 1.5 lakhs in a year.
Deductions under Section 80C 1
One of the striking features of Section 80C is that the deductions are available not only on the salary but the total income of the taxpayer, also called the gross income. This greatly helps in reducing the tax burden greatly. Keep in mind the following pointers:
These deductions can be availed only by individuals and HUFs, Hindu Undivided Families
Partnership Firms, companies, etc. cannot avail of these benefits
The total amount of INR 1.5 lakhs can be saved under Section 80C, 80CCC and 80CCD(1) combined
Let us understand these deductions in detail:
Life Insurance Premium3
You can claim a tax reduction U/S 80C for the premium paid for insurance coverage for yourself, your spouse, and your children.
PPF (Public Provident Fund)3
Public Provident Fund is a government-initiated plan where an individual can invest from INR 500 - INR 1,50,000 in a financial year. This investment amount can be claimed as a deduction under section 80C.
3.Senior Citizen Savings Plan2
Individuals who are above 60 years can invest up to INR 1,50,000 per financial year, and this entire amount is deductible under section 80C.
4.Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana2
If you have a girl child who is below 10 years, you can invest in the Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana scheme which allows tax deduction under section 80C. This facility can be availed for up to 2 girls.
Tax Saving FDs2
If you deposit in fixed deposits for a period of 5 or more years, you can claim for a tax deduction.
Equity-Linked Savings schemes (ELSS)3
If you wish to invest in equity funds, you can claim a deduction under Section 80C as per the limits stated in the policy.
Home Loan Repayment3
If you have taken a loan from any banking institution, you can claim up to INR 1,50,000 as a tax benefit.
Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) 3
ULIP is made of two components: You can claim up to INR 1,50,000 as a tax deduction under Section 80C for any premium paid under ULIP policy.
How to Maximize Tax Saving under Section 80C?
The tax saving investment options discussed above are ways to maximise tax savings. While building a corpus for a financially safe future, you also get to reduce the burden of taxes on your monthly budget. Post the Union Budget 2020-21, you have the option to choose the old tax regime and continue taking the tax benefits under Section 80C. Choose an appropriate product that acts as tax saving investment and helps you save more.
Conclusion
Under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, you get a plethora of solutions to reduce your tax liabilities by making tax saving investments. That's why it is imperative that you select the appropriate investment policies and instruments to increase your financial savings and security by tax savings.
Based on your earnings, expenses, and requirements, create a portfolio by integrating life insurance into it, as it offers huge life coverage and saves taxes. This will help you accomplish two goals in one step.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
References
https://cleartax.in/s/80c-80-deductions
https://cleartax.in/s/80c-deductions
https://www.outlookindia.com/business/best-tax-saving-investment-options-available-under-section-80c-news-192409

output.final_text:
Here Is Everything You Need to Know About Section 80C
For a taxpayer, Section 80C of Income tax Act 1961 is probably one of the relevant part while calculating taxes.. While 80C is quite popular, there are many aspects that one may not be completely aware of. Understanding all the deductions in detail can affect your tax exemptions to an extent and can help you save taxes on an amount up to INR 1.5 lakhs in a year. Deductions under Section 80C 1
One of the striking features of Section 80C of Income tax Act 1961 is that the deductions are available not only on the salary but the total income of the taxpayer, also called the gross income. This greatly helps in reducing the tax burden. Consider following pointers regarding Section 80 C:
● These deductions can be availed only by individuals and Hindu Undivided Families (“HUFs”) ● Partnership Firms, companies, etc. cannot avail of these benefits ● Taxes on an amount of up to INR 1.5 lakhs can be saved under Section 80C, 80CCC and 80CCD(1) combined Let us understand these deductions in detail:
1. Life Insurance Premium3
You can claim a tax deduction U/S 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961, for the premium paid for insurance coverage for yourself, your spouse, and your children subject to provisions stated therein. 2. PPF (Public Provident Fund)3
Public Provident Fund is a government-initiated plan where an individual can invest from INR 500 - INR 1,50,000 in a financial year. This investment amount can be claimed as a deduction under section 80C. 3.Senior Citizen Savings Plan2
Individuals who are above 60 years can invest up to INR 1,50,000 per financial year, and this entire amount is deductible under section 80C. 4. Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana2
If you have a girl child who is below 10 years, you can invest in the Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana scheme which allows tax deduction under section 80C subject to provisions stated in the Act. 5. Tax Saving FDs2
If you deposit in fixed deposits for a period of 5 or more years, you can claim for a tax deduction. 6. Equity-Linked Savings schemes (ELSS)3
If you wish to invest in equity funds, you can claim a deduction under Section 80C as per the
limits stated in the policy. 7. Home Loan Repayment3
If you have taken a home loan from any banking institution, you can claim up to INR 1,50,000 as a tax benefit on the principal amount. However, this can be claimed only when conditions stated in the Income Tax Act 1961 for claiming this deduction are met. 8. Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) 3
: You can claim premiums paid towards ULIPs for an amount up to INR 1,50,000 as a deduction from gross income under Section 80C. How to Maximize Tax Saving under Section 80C? The tax saving investment options discussed above are ways to maximise tax savings. While building a corpus for a financially safe future, you also get to reduce the burden of taxes on your monthly budget. As per the Union Budget 2022, you can opt for the old tax regime and continue taking tax benefits under Section 80C4. Choose an appropriate product that acts as tax saving investment and helps you save more. Conclusion Under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961, you get a number of solutions to reduce your tax liabilities by making tax saving investments. That's why you may consider making investments in appropriate instruments to increase your financial savings and security by tax savings. Based on your earnings, expenses, and requirements, you may consider creating a portfolio by integrating life insurance into it, as it offers life coverage and saves taxes. This will help you accomplish two goals in one step. Disclaimer: IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER.
